clinical_coherence.notes: Flawless clinical picture. 28F with anxiety, depression, IBS, childhood trauma history presenting with right leg weakness and PNES. Positive Hoover sign, give-way weakness, midline splitting, non-anatomic sensory loss, and PNES captured on video-EEG with no ictal correlate. All classic positive functional signs present. Psychiatric comorbidities and psychosocial stressor (recent separation) are realistic predisposing and precipitating factors.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG normal with three captured events showing no ictal correlate, confirming PNES. MRI normal. Labs unremarkable (minor sodium 132 is clinically insignificant). Post-event prolactin normal, supporting non-epileptic events. All tool outputs perfectly concordant with FND diagnosis.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD F44.4 is correct for conversion disorder with motor symptoms. Differential is comprehensive and well-reasoned. Critical actions include identifying positive signs, video-EEG, empathic diagnosis delivery, and levetiracetam discontinuation. Contraindicated actions are appropriate.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Straightforward is appropriate. Classic presentation with abundant positive functional signs (Hoover, give-way, midline splitting, PNES on EEG), clear psychosocial precipitant, and textbook demographics. An agent should identify this with moderate confidence.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 28F with anxiety, depression, and childhood trauma is the prototypical FND demographic. BMI 24.1, medications (sertraline, dicyclomine, ibuprofen) all appropriate. Family history of fibromyalgia in mother adds realistic familial somatization tendency.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding teaching case demonstrating positive diagnostic approach to FND, the importance of Hoover sign, video-EEG for PNES confirmation, normal post-event prolactin, and the role of empathic diagnosis communication. Comprehensive multidisciplinary treatment plan included.

strengths: Textbook positive functional signs with clear clinical descriptions
Video-EEG capturing PNES events with detailed semiology
Comprehensive follow-up outputs including EMG/NCS, spine MRI, neuropsych, and psychiatric evaluation
Well-structured ground truth with detailed key reasoning points
